example: The politician's incendiary remarks sparked violent protests.
mnemonic: '인센디어리' → 'incend(불 붙이다)+iary' 불을 붙이듯 선동적인!

example: His pedantic insistence on grammar rules made conversations exhausting.
mnemonic: ped(가르치다, 그리스어 paideuein) + antic = 과도하게 가르치려 하는 → 현학적인. ped(아이, 교육): pedagogy

example: The idea had been gestating in her mind for years before she wrote the novel.
mnemonic: gest(나르다, 라틴어 gestare) + ate = 안에 품고 다니다 → 임신하다, 구상하다. gest(나르다): digest, ingest

example: Firefighters worked for hours to extricate the driver from the wreckage.
mnemonic: '엑스트리케이트' → 'ex(밖으로)+tric(얽힌)+ate' 얽힘에서 빼내어 구출!

example: It may seem counterintuitive, but exercising actually gives you more energy.
mnemonic: counter(반대) + intuitive(직관적인) = 직관에 반하는. intuit(안쪽에서 보다): intuition

example: Currency fluctuations can significantly affect international trade.
mnemonic: fluct(흐르다, 라틴어 fluctuare=물결치다) + uation = 물결처럼 오르내리는 것 → 변동. flu(흐르다): fluid, fluent, flux

example: The abacus was a precursor to the modern computer.
mnemonic: pre(미리) + curs(달리다) + or = 미리 달리는 사람 → 선구자. curs(달리다): cursor, current, occur

example: The defense attorney impugned the credibility of the prosecution's witness.
mnemonic: '임퓨언' → 'im(향해)+pugn(싸우다)' 향해 싸우며 이의 제기!

example: The garish neon signs made the street look cheap and tacky.
mnemonic: 고대 영어 gauren(응시하다). 응시하게 만들 정도로 → 야한, 화려한

example: The extreme heat enervated the hikers, forcing them to rest frequently.
mnemonic: '에너베이트' → 'e+nerve(신경)+ate' 신경을 빼앗아 기력 약화!

example: The poem contains many allusions to Greek mythology.
mnemonic: al(~쪽으로) + lus(놀다, 라틴어 ludere) + ion = 간접적으로 가지고 놀다 → 암시. lud/lus(놀다): illusion, elude, ludicrous

example: The novel uses an omniscient narrator who knows every character's thoughts.
mnemonic: omni(모든) + sci(알다, 라틴어 scire) + ent = 모든 것을 아는 → 전지의. sci(알다): science, conscious, prescient

example: Historians often point to decadence as a factor in the fall of Rome.
mnemonic: de(아래로) + cad(떨어지다, 라틴어 cadere) + ence = 아래로 떨어짐 → 쇠퇴. cad/cas(떨어지다): cascade, cadence, occasion

example: Paris is the quintessential romantic city for many travelers.
mnemonic: quint(다섯째) + essential(본질적) = 고대 철학의 제5원소(정수) → 전형적인, 정수의

example: The intransigent union refused to accept any of the company's proposals.
mnemonic: '인트랜시전트' → 'in(아닌)+transig(타협)+ent' 타협하지 않는 완고한!

example: Young voters are often criticized for being apathetic about politics.
mnemonic: a(없는) + path(감정) + etic = 감정이 없는 → 무관심한. path(감정): apathy, sympathy, empathy, pathetic

example: The propinquity of the two nations led to frequent cultural exchanges.
mnemonic: propin(가까운, 라틴어 propinquus) + quity = 가까움 → 근접. prope(가까운): approximate, propinquus, proximity와 의미 유사

example: The noisome odor from the factory made the neighborhood unlivable.
mnemonic: noi(해, annoy의 어근) + some = 해로운 → 불쾌한. annoy(짜증나게 하다)와 같은 어근. noise(소음)과 관련 없음

example: The dour librarian silenced anyone who dared to whisper.
mnemonic: 라틴어 durus(단단한, 엄격한)에서 유래. 단단하고 엄격한 → 음침한. dur(단단한): durable, endure, obdurate

example: Climate change presents a conundrum that requires global cooperation.
mnemonic: 라틴어 유사형태. con(함께) + 어원 불확실하나 16세기 학술 용어에서 유래. 복잡하게 뒤엉킨 난제, 수수께끼

example: The confluence of the two rivers creates a spectacular landscape.
mnemonic: con(함께) + flu(흐르다) + ence = 함께 흐르는 것 → 합류. flu(흐르다): fluent, affluent, influence, fluid

example: The arts flourished under the patronage of wealthy nobles.
mnemonic: patron(후원자, 라틴어 patronus=보호자) + age = 후원하는 것 → 후원. patr(아버지): patriarch, paternal

example: The impecunious artist could barely afford to buy paint and canvas.
mnemonic: im(없는) + pecuni(돈, 라틴어 pecunia) + ous = 돈이 없는 → 무일푼의. pecun(돈): pecuniary(금전의)

example: The government imposed austerity measures to reduce debt.
mnemonic: auster(엄격한, 건조한, 그리스어 austeros) + ity = 엄격함 → 긴축. austere에서 파생

example: The strange phenomena are not easily explicable by current science.
mnemonic: '엑스플리커블' → 'ex+plic(펴다)+able' 펼쳐서 설명할 수 있는!

example: Her erudition in classical literature impressed all the conference attendees.
mnemonic: erud(가르치다, 라틴어 erudire=다듬다) + ition = 많이 배운 상태 → 박식. erudite(박식한)

example: The new law enfranchised millions of citizens who had been denied the right to vote.
mnemonic: en(만들다) + franchise(자유, 프랑스어 franc=자유로운) = 자유롭게 하다 → 참정권을 주다

example: The lawsuit alleged copyright infringement by the tech company.
mnemonic: in(안으로) + fringe(가장자리를 부수다, 라틴어 frangere) + ment = 경계를 부수고 들어감 → 침해. frang/fract(부수다): fraction, fracture

example: The capacious auditorium can seat over two thousand people.
mnemonic: capac(잡다, 담다, 라틴어 capax) + ious = 많이 담을 수 있는 → 넓은. cap(잡다, 담다): capacity, capable, capture

example: She was kindness incarnate, always helping those in need.
mnemonic: in(안에) + carn(살, 라틴어 caro) + ate = 살 안에 들어간 → 화신의. carn(살): carnal, carnivore, carnival

example: The author's posthumous novel was published two years after her death.
mnemonic: post(후에) + hum(땅, 라틴어 humus) + ous = 땅에 묻힌 후의 → 사후의. hum(땅): humble, human, exhume

example: She presented a cogent argument that convinced the entire jury.
mnemonic: co(함께) + gent(이끌다, 라틴어 agere) = 함께 이끄는 → 설득력 있는. ag/act(행하다, 이끌다): agent, cogitate

example: The rebel leader fomented unrest among the dissatisfied workers.
mnemonic: '포멘트' → 'foam(거품)+ment' 거품이 일듯 불만을 조장!

example: He appeared contrite after realizing the harm his words had caused.
mnemonic: con(완전히) + trit(갈다, 라틴어 terere) + e = 마음이 완전히 갈린 → 뉘우치는. trit(갈다): attrition(마모), trite(진부한=닳은)

example: His laconic reply of just one word left everyone wanting more details.
mnemonic: 라코니아(Laconia) 사람들이 말이 적기로 유명했던 것에서 유래 → 간결한

ko: 뚫을 수 없는, 이해할 수 없는
example: The fortress was surrounded by impenetrable walls.
mnemonic: im(아닌) + penetr(뚫다) + able = 뚫을 수 없는. penetr(뚫다): penetrate, penetrating

example: The wily fox managed to evade the hunters once again.
mnemonic: wile(계략, 속임수) + y = 계략을 쓰는 → 교활한

example: The manager was amenable to the employees' suggestions.
mnemonic: a(~쪽으로) + men(이끌다, 라틴어 minare) + able = 이끌릴 수 있는 → 수용적인. min(이끌다): menace(위협하여 이끌다)

example: The war left an indelible mark on the nation's collective memory.
mnemonic: '인델러블' → 'in(아닌)+del(지우다)+ible' 지울 수 없는!

example: Blood coagulates when exposed to air to form a protective clot.
mnemonic: co(함께) + agul(몰다, 라틴어 agere) + ate = 함께 몰아 뭉치다 → 응고하다

example: The ubiquity of smartphones has transformed how people communicate.
mnemonic: ubique(어디에나, 라틴어) + ity = 어디에나 있는 상태 → 편재. ubiquitous(어디에나 있는)

example: Children have a higher susceptibility to certain infectious diseases.
mnemonic: sus(아래에서) + cept(잡다) + ibility = 아래에서 잡힐 수 있는 → 취약성. susceptible(취약한)의 명사형

example: The garden offered a plenitude of colorful flowers in every season.
mnemonic: plen(가득한, 라틴어 plenus) + itude = 가득 찬 상태 → 풍부. plen(가득): plenty, plenary, replenish

example: The debate devolved into personal invective rather than policy discussion.
mnemonic: in(향해) + vect(운반하다) + ive = 비난을 퍼붓다. vect(운반): vector, convey

example: Interest accrues on the savings account every month.
mnemonic: ac(~쪽으로) + crue(자라다, 라틴어 crescere) = 점점 자라나다 → 누적되다. cresc/cre 어근: increase, crescendo

example: The oil magnate donated billions to philanthropic causes.
mnemonic: magn(큰, 라틴어 magnus) + ate = 큰 사람 → 거물. magn(큰): magnificent, magnitude, magnify

example: Her histrionic reaction to the minor inconvenience annoyed everyone.
mnemonic: histrion(배우, 라틴어 histrio) + ic = 배우 같은 → 연극적인, 과장된

example: She tried to appease the angry customer with a full refund.
mnemonic: ap(~쪽으로) + pease(평화, 라틴어 pax) = 평화 쪽으로 이끌다 → 달래다. pac/pax(평화): pacify, pacific

example: The evidence against the suspect was unassailable.
mnemonic: un(아닌) + assail(공격하다) + able = 공격할 수 없는 → 난공불락의. sail(뛰어오르다): assail, assault

example: The philanthropic foundation donates millions to medical research each year.
mnemonic: phil(사랑, 그리스어) + anthrop(인간) + ic = 인간을 사랑하는 → 박애주의의. phil(사랑): philosophy. anthrop(인간): anthropology
